codeReview.summary: The feature delivers substantial UI/backend scaffolding for scheduled changes, but it misses core correctness and security requirements: due-change application is not automatic and the apply endpoint allows unauthorized cross-org mutations.

issues.description: POST /api/scheduled-changes/apply-due is only protected by auth and has no org/scenario role guard. Any authenticated user can trigger applying scheduled changes.
applyDueChanges queries and applies all pending scheduled changes globally (no org/scenario/user scoping). Combined with the unguarded route, one user can mutate employee data outside their org.

Auto-apply is not actually automatic. The implementation only exposes a manual endpoint; there is no background job or middleware invocation path (server references to applyDueChanges are only the controller export and route mount), so VAL-ORG-014 behavior is not met.

effectiveDate is validated only as a non-empty string. Invalid date strings are not rejected up front before Date parsing, which can lead to cast/runtime errors instead of deterministic 400 validation responses.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission/server guidance emphasizes auth middleware but does not explicitly codify that mutating scenario/org endpoints must enforce role-scoped authorization middleware. This gap likely contributed to the unguarded apply-due write route.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md:29-31 and implementation-worker SKILL.md:38 describe route pattern as auth middleware; compare to packages/server/src/routes/scheduledChangeRoutes.ts:25 where a mutating endpoint is mounted without requireScenarioRole/requireOrgRole.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared architecture documentation is stale for this milestone and does not include ScheduledChange entities/data flow, making it harder for future workers to reason about required invariants (scoping, automatic application semantics).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:36-43 lists User→Organization→Scenario→Employee data model only, with no ScheduledChange model or apply-due flow.

summary: Review failed due to blocking security and behavior gaps: apply-due is globally writable by any authenticated user and auto-application is not implemented as an automatic mechanism.
